options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: ForgeTheCrystal
options.description:
Bring the Adamant and Legend Sword to clear this objective.
Forces the objective reward to be the Crystal.

options.name: ConquerTheGiant
options.description:
Clear the Giant of Bab-il to clear this objective.
No character will be available from the Giant with this objective.

options.name: DefeatTheFiends
options.description:
Defeat every elemental fiend to clear this objective.
Your targets are Milon, Milon Z., Kainazzo, Valvalis, Rubicant, and the Elements bosses. Good hunting.

options.name: FindTheDarkMatter
options.description:
Find thirty Dark Matters and deliver them to Kory in Agart to clear this objective.
There are forty-five Dark Matters in total.

options.name: AdditionalObjectives
options.description:
The number of additional random objectives. Can be quests, boss fights, or character recruitments. Note that
no matter what this is set to, no more than thirty-two objectives will be set.

options.name: RequiredObjectiveCount
options.description:
The number of objectives required for victory. Note that this is ignored when no objectives are set. If this
count is greater than the total number of objectives available, then it will be reduced to match the number of
available objectives.

options.name: ObjectiveReward
options.description:
The reward for clearing all objectives. Note that this is ignored when no objectives are set,
and Forge the Crystal forces this to the Crystal setting.

options.name: NoFreeCharacters
options.description:
If set, characters will not be available at locations with no requirements or bosses. These locations are
Mysidia, Damcyan Watery Pass, and Mt. Ordeals.

options.name: NoEarnedCharacters
options.description:
If set, characters will not be available at locations with requirements or bosses. These locations are Mist,
Kaipo, Mt. Hobs, Baron, the Tower of Zot, Cave Eblana, Lunar Palace, and the Giant of Bab-il.

options.name: AllowedCharacters
options.description: Pool of characters allowed to show up. Note that if Hero Challenge is enabled, your hero will still appear.

options.name: EnsureAllCharacters
options.description: Ensure at least one instance of each allowed character is available, if possible.

options.name: AllowDuplicateCharacters
options.description: Allows multiple instances of the same character to join your party.

options.name: RestrictedCharacters
options.description: List of characters that can't appear in the easiest to access locations if possible.

options.name: ItemPlacement
options.description:
Where items can and will be placed.
Setting this to Full Shuffle will allow any items to be anywhere.
Setting this to Major Minor Split will force all non-major locations to never have progression.
In either case, major locations can only have useful or progression items.
Major locations are any MIAB or event locations.

options.name: EnableDefaultPriorityLocations
options.description:
If set, major event locations and Monster-In-A-Box locations will be guaranteed to have
useful or progression items.

options.name: PassEnabled
options.description: Will the Pass be included in the Key Item Pool?

options.name: PassInShops
options.description: Can the pass show up in shops? This is a convenience feature and will never be required by the logic.

options.name: ItemRandomization
options.description: Affects item pool

options.name: MinTier
options.description: The minimum tier of items that can appear in the item pool.

options.name: MaxTier
options.description: The maximum tier of items that can appear in the item pool.

options.name: JunkTier
options.description: Items of this tier or below will automatically be sold for cold hard cash.

options.name: JItems
options.description:
Affects whether items not in the US release of FF4 will appear in shops or the item pool.
Does not affect enemy drops/steals.

options.name: JunkedItems
options.description: Items that will always be sold for GP regardless of your junk tier settings.

options.name: KeptItems
options.description: Items that will never be sold for GP regardless of your junk tier settings. Takes priority over Junked Items.

options.name: HeroChallenge
options.description:
Enable the Hero Challenge. In Hero Challenge, your starting character is your main character and cannot be
dismissed. They will face the top of Mt. Ordeals on their own, and Kokkol will forge a weapon from FFIV Advance
for them (unless Forge the Crystal is set).

options.name: UnsafeKeyItemPlacement
options.description:
Normally, underground access is guaranteed to be available without taking a trip to the moon.
Toggling this on disables this check.

options.name: PartySize
options.description: Maximum party size.

options.name: CharactersPermajoin
options.description: If enabled, characters may not be dismissed from the party.

options.name: CharactersPermadie
options.description:
If enabled, characters petrified or dead at the end of battle will be removed from your party forever.
On Extreme difficulty, this also includes "cutscene" fights (Mist, Dark Elf, etc.)

options.name: NoAdamantArmors
options.description: Remove Adamant Armor from the item and shop pool.

options.name: KeepDoorsBehemoths
options.description: Should Trap Door and Behemoth Fights be enabled even when encounters are off?

options.name: NoFreeBosses
options.description: Removes alternate win conditions for bosses other than good old fashioned violence.

options.name: WackyChallenge
options.description:
Wacky challenges are not fair, balanced, stable, or even necessarily interesting.
They are, however, quite wacky. See FE documentation for more info, or pick one for a fun surprise!

choices.name: the_floor_is_made_of_lava
choices.display_name: The Floor Is Made Of Lava

options.name: MIABRandomization
options.description:
Affects where MIABs are randomized to and their contents.
Randomized will randomize the location of MIAB chests, while Vanilla keeps them in their default location.
Vanilla Exclude acts as vanilla, but progression will never be placed in their locations.

options.name: ShopRandomization
options.description: Affects the placement of items in shops. See FE documentation for more for now.

options.name: FreeShops
options.description: Everything must go!

options.name: StarterKitOne
options.description: FE Starter Kit 1. See FE Documentation for details. Or just pick one, they can't hurt you.

options.name: StarterKitTwo
options.description: FE Starter Kit 2. See FE Documentation for details. Or just pick one, they can't hurt you.

options.name: StarterKitThree
options.description: FE Starter Kit 3. See FE Documentation for details. Or just pick one, they can't hurt you.

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: item_links
options.description: Share part of your item pool with other players.

options.name: plando_items
options.description: Generic items plando.

options.name: start_inventory_from_pool
options.description:
Start with the specified amount of these items and don't place them in the world. Example: "Bomb: 1"

The game decides what the replacement items will be.
